item.title: Fed’s Williams Signals Steady Rates in Face of War’s Risks
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams reiterated monetary policy remains well positioned to deal with the threat of a protracted supply shock caused by war in the Middle East that could raise inflation and dampen growth in the US.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Fall to 207,000, Signaling Low Layoffs
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week, indicating that layoffs remain largely limited.

item.title: Canva Leans Into AI to Defend Its $42 Billion Empire
item.description: Australia’s highest-profile tech unicorn is undertaking a risky transformation as it scrambles to prove its relevance in the age of generative AI.

item.title: Roku’s Platform Crosses 100 Million Users, Fueling Ad Business
item.description: Roku Inc. said more than 100 million households are using its streaming platform, marking another milestone as the company continues to leverage that customer base to fuel its growing advertising business.

item.title: Stock Rally Leaves Options Traders Readying for a Tech Rebound
item.description: A dramatic shift is underway in US equity markets. Stocks hit an all-time high Wednesday as Middle East peace hopes tamped down geopolitical anxiety, and derivatives traders who had pulled back from bullish bets are now racing to position for further gains in technology shares.

item.title: Brazil Activity Beats Forecast as Economy Weathers High Rates
item.description: Brazil’s economic activity accelerated slightly faster than expected in February, underscoring the economy’s solid growth in the face of double-digit borrowing costs.

item.title: UAE Investor Buys Stake in General Atlantic’s Joe & the Juice
item.description: Emirates International Investment Co. has acquired a minority stake in Joe & the Juice in a transaction valuing the General Atlantic-backed company at $1.8 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cash-Strapped Schools Are Paying for AI to Help Fix Finances
item.description: Florida’s Broward County Public Schools is currently facing a budget shortfall of $100 million, the largest deficit caused by annual student enrollment declines.

item.title: Stellantis Inks Artificial Intelligence Deal With Microsoft
item.description: Stellantis NV is teaming up with Microsoft Corp. to accelerate deployment of artificial intelligence software across its cars and operations to improve customer experience.

item.title: Kremlin’s War Leaves Russia With Some of World’s Costliest Money
item.description: Russia’s tight monetary policy has landed it in an unlikely club of countries usually comprised of economies battling debt distress or runaway inflation, laying bare the economic costs from the war in Ukraine beyond the swelling military-spending bill.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Hims & Hers, PPG Industries, QuidelOrtho
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.1% as of 7:51 a.m. in New York as an upbeat forecast from TSMC lifts technology shares.

item.title: Benin Sees IMF Agreement as Top Priority For New Government
item.description: Benin’s newly elected government plans to seek a new International Monetary Fund program when it enters office in May, as it looks to sustain economic growth that’s made it an outlier among peers.

item.title: Wall Street Quants See an Edge in Polymarket Earnings Forecasts
item.description: Every quarter, Wall Street’s hordes of analysts engineer financial models, parse alternative data and jostle for access to executives as they attempt to predict company earnings. New research suggests the anonymous bettors on Polymarket might give them a run for their money.

item.title: Google Told to Share Search Data With AI Rivals in EU Proposal
item.description: The European Union proposed measures to open up key Google data to rival search engines, including artificial intelligence chatbots, as the bloc seeks to rein in the power of the biggest US technology companies.

item.title: Bondholders Seek Sweeteners as Shapoorji Unit Pushes Extension
item.description: Bondholders in a unit of India’s Shapoorji Pallonji Group are asking for compensation after the construction conglomerate sought a two-month extension to repay its debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Aluminum Market Enters a Prolonged Supply ‘Black Hole’
item.description: JPMorgan has been warning of a “serious and prolonged” outage that it says has now arrived.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Dives Into AI to Build a Zuckbot
item.description: The Meta CEO is training an artificial intelligence assistant to handle some of his duties

item.title: The US Oil Industry Doesn’t Want the Iran War Either
item.description: American producers are uncomfortable about being viewed as instruments of Donald Trump’s foreign policy.

item.title: Bessent Signals Potential Cover for Warsh to Stand Pat on Rates
item.description: Twice in two days, Bessent said he would understand it if the Fed doesn’t proceed with reducing rates as it awaits more information about the impact the Iran war is having on the US.

item.title: ‘Hunger Bonds’ Comeback Shows Depth of Venezuela Debt Rally
item.description: Venezuelan bonds that became a global symbol of Wall Street’s indifference to the country’s deep economic crisis are now among the biggest winners in a sweeping debt rally.

item.title: Global Trade Policy Reacts Swiftly to Iran War Disruptions
item.description: List of measures from fuel subsidies to export controls has reached 250 and is still growing

item.title: German Companies Increasingly Favor Asia Over US Investments
item.description: Fewer German companies are investing in the US due to President Donald Trump’s trade tariffs and instead shift their focus to China and other Asian countries, according to a survey by industry lobby group DIHK.

item.title: Turkey to Improve Transport Network as It Eyes Europe-Gulf Route
item.description: Turkey, Syria and Jordan have agreed to modernize their railway systems with the aim of eventually creating a contiguous corridor between southern Europe and the Persian Gulf, according to Turkey’s transportation minister.

item.title: ECB to Scrutinize Anthropic’s Mythos on Call With Executives
item.description: The European Central Bank is convening a call with the chief risk officers of eurozone lenders to discuss the potential threats from Anthropic’s new artificial intelligence model, as officials look to understand the technology’s potential to exploit vulnerabilities in financial systems.

item.title: EDP Freezes Three US Wind Projects Over Trump-Era Policy Risks
item.description: EDP SA has paused three US wind projects due to limited policy visibility under the Trump administration, underscoring how regulatory uncertainty is weighing on renewable energy investment decisions.

item.title: Foundering: The True Story of Bob Lee’s Killer
item.description: San Francisco officials were eager to quash the doom loop narrative and insisted that Bob Lee was killed by someone he knew. But Lee’s friends and family had never heard of his accused murderer, Nima Momeni.

item.title: BNY Profit Beats Analyst Estimates on Revenue Gains
item.description: Bank of New York Mellon Corp. posted profit that beat analyst estimates as client activity fueled revenue gains during a busy quarter.

item.title: Netflix Earnings Shift Focus to Basics as ‘M&A Overhang’ Clears
item.description: Netflix Inc.’s stock struggled through the winter as the company engaged in a bidding war for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. But since ending the chase two months ago, the company has gotten back to basics, a strategy welcomed by investors and that’s expected to be validated when it reports earnings after the close.

item.title: Tax Refund Splurge for Many Americans Is Paying Down Debt
item.description: Households use this year’s bigger refunds to trim debt, pay bills — and treat themselves with what’s left.

item.title: Foundering: Tech Executive’s Mysterious Death Prompts San Francisco Doom Loop Panic
item.description: When Bob Lee, the founder of Cash App, was killed in the streets of San Francisco, his death was initially a mystery. The city took the blame.
